notes: https://pomb.us/build-your-own-react/. Canonical React-from-scratch tutorial. Fiber, reconciliation, hooks. Directly applicable — React 19 in SoNash stack.

notes: Feynman principle applied to system understanding. Structure JASON-OS Internal Archaeology (Domain 01) as 'how each system was built' narratives rather than 'what exists' inventories.

prepare.py (don't touch) + train.py (agent edits) + program.md (human edits). Clean editable-zone contract.
Read stack trace, attempt fix, revert if unfixable. Simpler than checkpoint+resume for low-cost tasks.
commit/metric/memory/status/description. Already have JSONL equivalent. Low extraction value.

Webhook for real-time capable services, polling for those without. Park for event-driven agent features.
moviepy frame extraction + FAISS/OpenSearch similarity dedup. Configurable FPS, 10min chunks. Claims 50% frame reduction. Core pattern for T27.
Three-level temporal decomposition. Each level has summaries, timestamps, metadata. Structures video content analysis.
Each ML feature independently enabled via config. Adding new capabilities = adding new function to pipeline. Extensibility pattern.

52 Python files, 1 test. AWS reference architectures skip tests. Don't follow this pattern.

Start job -> poll -> fetch transcript -> cleanup. Speaker labels, S3 output routing. T27 speech-to-text reference.

1.5K stars, MIT. Built-in SRT caption extraction, playlist/channel enumeration, search API with filters, AsyncYouTube. Caption extraction may eliminate Whisper/Transcribe for captioned videos. T27 workhorse.

time.sleep(30) polling loop for Transcribe. Fine for CLI, must not copy into agent pipelines.
Complete local Whisper lifecycle: CUDA detection, large-v3, beam_size=10, vad_filter, segment iteration with tqdm, metadata capture. T27 reference.
beam_size=10 (vs default 5) for accuracy. vad_filter=True prevents hallucination-on-silence. Production tuning.
Cross-repo insight: both bedrock and this repo skip captions. pytubefix has yt.captions. Caption-first for ~80% of videos, Whisper fallback for rest.
asyncio.Semaphore(4) for concurrent downloads, sequential GPU transcription. Network parallelizes, GPU doesn't.
CTranslate2 Whisper. 4x faster than OpenAI whisper. GPU+CPU. THE local transcription engine for T27.
numba.cuda.is_available(), Anaconda toolkit paths, float16 vs auto compute. Real-world GPU setup.

$0.006/min API (less accurate) vs free local (more accurate, needs GPU). 1h = $0.36 vs $0.

280 lines, no CLI, no config file. Works for personal use, impossible to integrate. T27 must be modular.

Module-level variables including API keys. No CLI args, no env vars.

Raw folder staging → LLM wiki → master index. Lightweight RAG alternative. Our .research/ pipeline is the evolved version.

Pluggable extraction engines coordinated by a fallback-list builder. buildFallbackList → scrapeURLWithEngine → (success?) → try next or NoEnginesLeftError. Named failure mode, documented order, engine-level telemetry.
Gate tests by runtime capability (env vars) not by CI fence. Same test file runs in all environments; gate decides whether assertions execute. Three lines per gate.
Single harness.ts script owns service boot order (API + workers + emulator) before test runner. CLAUDE.md mandates it; no ad-hoc `pnpm start` in dev docs.
Parallel v0/v1/v2 controller directories with their own test fixtures. New features land on v2; v0 stays alive for compat. Three OpenAPI specs frozen at repo root.
Use redlock.using([key], ttlMs, ...) to serialise cache updates across multiple pods. Prevents race conditions on shared cache slots (e.g. ACUC credit updates).
Reference implementation of multi-agent coordination around a single scraping task. Worth reading to inform SoNash agent teams (.claude/teams/) and the agent boundary design work for JASON-OS.

How firecrawl's Deep Research API is invoked in practice (apartment-finder example). Interactive preference gathering → research call → LLM analysis → structured JSON. Compare to SoNash /deep-research skill's searcher pipeline.

Repo-walking via firecrawl Map API, same domain as SoNash repo-analysis.

Docs-to-OpenAPI extraction pattern. Informs SoNash document-analysis.
19-line CLAUDE.md that beats most 200-line variants. Test-first, E2E preferred, capability-gating, harness usage. Worth a direct comparison against SoNash's 235-line CLAUDE.md for signal density.

1162 files, one mermaid diagram, no docs/ directory. Queue layout, auth flow, service boundaries all have to be inferred from code. New contributors pay the ramp-up tax. SoNash should file this as evidence in the next 'do we need this ADR?' conversation.

candidate: Two queue systems coexisting without migration plan
notes: BullMQ (Redis) and nuq-postgres (custom Postgres queue) both active in services/. Rationale undocumented. Suggests in-progress migration with no exit criteria. If SoNash ever needs a queue, pick one and commit — don't run two.

Seven SDKs (JS, Python, Rust, Go, Java, Elixir, playwright-service) re-implement the wire contract manually. Parity enforced by OpenAPI specs + test-suite app. Visible tax: adding an endpoint is a 7-file change. Correct answer for SoNash if ever pressured: generate from OpenAPI or say no.

candidate: Hidden feature flags via env vars
notes: Firecrawl branches behaviour through SELF_HOST.md env vars (USE_DB_AUTHENTICATION, PROXY_SERVER, SEARXNG_*) with no documented feature-flag layer. Fine at small-team scale, breaks down as team grows. Prefer explicit feature flags over env-var branching for user-visible behaviour.

docs/en/reference/output_files.md is an exemplary human-readable output spec: typed fields, enumerated block types, breaking-change callouts between pipeline and VLM backends. Template for creating docs/agent_docs/ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_CONTRACT.md to mirror SoNash CAS v3.0 schema. Half-day writing effort that would pay forward through every future schema migration.

docs/en/usage/cli_tools.md has prose catalog of 15+ MINERU_* env vars with default/effect/scoping. Template for docs/agent_docs/ENV_VARS.md to consolidate SoNash scattered SKIP_*/GSD_*/CAS_*/ANALYZE_* across hooks, scripts, and skills. E0 effort — inventory + prose writing.

candidate: Async task state machine with TTL cleanup
notes: mineru/cli/fast_api.py implements POST /tasks with four states (pending/processing/completed/failed), asyncio.Queue, semaphore concurrency cap, background TTL sweep. Direct answer to T28 Gap Agent A Q5 (progressive extraction). SoNash state files already have the task-record shape — missing pieces are state machine + sweep. AGPL source blocks direct lift; conceptual transfer only. Blocks on decision whether SoNash wants progressive extraction.

candidate: Backend decision matrix for multi-mode tools
notes: docs/en/quick_start/index.md has pre-install decision matrix: 5 backends × 8 constraints. Apply to /analyze and /media-analysis where users pick source type + depth + backend (whisper/oEmbed/transcript).

mineru/cli/common.py uses string-dispatched factory with importlib deferred imports so users don't pay torch/vllm cost unless they picked that backend. Duck-typed doc_analyze/aio_doc_analyze signatures across all engines. Reference pattern for skill families routing across heavy alternative implementations.

candidate: Single fuzzy-match e2e test with low coverage floor
notes: 58k-star codebase with 1 test file, 1 test function, 0.2% coverage floor, no PR gating, silent retry workflow masking flake. Guard against: never accept 'there's already an e2e test' as coverage during SoNash code-reviewer/test-engineer work. Visible in MinerU's 160-issue backlog as parsing regressions.

candidate: Unarmored HTTP service with insecure defaults
notes: mineru-api/router ship with no auth, no rate limit, no upload size cap, no CORS/TrustedHost middleware, FastAPI docs enabled by default. Docs encourage --host 0.0.0.0 for multi-GPU. Anti-pattern SoNash security-auditor exists to prevent. Guard: any SoNash HTTP service needs these defaults at construction, not operator-added.

candidate: Silent retry workflow converts flake into green
notes: .github/workflows/rerun.yml retries failed runs up to 3 times with no flake-rate surfacing. Opposite of silent-failure-hunter discipline. Guard: SoNash CI must never add automatic retry without flake surfacing.

cli.yml runs only on pushes to master/dev, never on pull requests. Contributors cannot see test results before merge. Guard: SoNash CI workflows must include pull_request triggers for any test-bearing workflow.

docs/en/reference/changelog.md has prose entries with issue refs, CVE refs, quantified perf improvements, explicit breaking-change callouts. Reading top-down gives a feature capability index. Reference pattern for any future SoNash public changelog.

candidate: Wrap upstream CLI instead of reimplementing
notes: mineru/model/vlm/vllm_server.py is 70 lines wrapping vllm.entrypoints.cli.main: intercepts sys.argv, parses out model/port/gpu-memory-utilization/logits-processors, applies mod_kwargs_by_device_type, auto-injects MinerULogitsProcessor, sets OMP_NUM_THREADS=1, hands off to vllm_main(). Cheap maintainable alternative to forking. Directly applicable to any SoNash need to wrap a third-party CLI with project-specific defaults.

11 Dockerfiles (1 global + 10 China vendor variants) following strict template: vendor base image -> Noto CJK fonts -> pip install mineru[core] with numpy + opencv pinned -> mineru-models-download. Each variant can carry vendor-specific workarounds inline. Reference for projects shipping to heterogeneous hardware. Not directly applicable to SoNash.

13 per-chip deployment guides follow consistent template: test platform -> Dockerfile recipe -> docker run -> support matrix -> tips. Outliers: AMD.md is community Triton patches (only doc with code), VastAI.md pinned to 2.7.0 with vlm-only support. Structural discipline is high but content is Chinese-only (see anti-mineru-004).

MinerU 2.5 paper (arxiv 2509.22186) introduces a 1.2B VLM with stage-1 layout analysis on downsampled images + stage-2 content recognition on native-resolution crops. Framing analogue for SoNash depth tiers (Quick -> Standard -> Deep): cheap structural pass first, spend resources only where it matters.

candidate: README integration claim unbacked by first-party code
notes: MinerU README lists MCP Server - Cursor/Claude Desktop/Windsurf as an integration. Reality: opendatalab/mineru-mcp does not exist (404). Top community implementation is neosun100/mineru-mcp-server (9 stars, no license). Guard: SoNash README and skill files must distinguish first-party from community integrations. If claimed in a table, must link to code SoNash maintains.

.github/workflows/mkdocs.yml uses mhausenblas/mkdocs-deploy-gh-pages@master (unpinned) while cla.yml pins contributor-assistant/github-action@v2.6.1. Inconsistent. Guard: all SoNash GitHub Actions must pin to version or SHA, never @master.

candidate: Floor-only version constraint with known CVE exposure
notes: pyproject.toml pins pillow>=11.0.0 with no upper bound. CVE-2026-25990 (Pillow PSD OOB write) affects 10.3.0 to <12.1.1. Floor is below the fix version, so exposure depends on resolver choice. Guard: SoNash deps with known CVE histories need ranged constraints, not floor-only.
arxiv 2409.18839 - 18 authors, PDF-Extract-Kit + pre/post-processing rules. Background reading only. Low direct relevance - we do not train extraction models.

arxiv 2509.22186 - 61 authors, 1.2B VLM, coarse-to-fine two-stage parsing. Low direct relevance but the two-stage framing applies to SoNash depth tiers (promoted to kno-mineru-009). Worth reading if working on the Standard vs Deep depth boundary design.
Zero-schema MCP server introspecting Click CLI. ~200 lines. Principle portable to JASON-OS Domain 02a (Node/TS adaptation needed).
Event-family naming, 2-digit ordering, foreground/background with SIGTERM. Complements SoNash hook governance.
498-line CLAUDE.md with grep-friendly naming, minimize-unique-names, NO MOCKS testing. Compare against SoNash 135-line CLAUDE.md.
JSON+jq pipeline for 0% coverage files. Passive coverage during dev server. Relevant to T21 orphan detection.
claude.yml GitHub Actions workflow. AI-in-CI pattern for JASON-OS CI/CD domain.
27K stars, 0/29 changesets reviewed. Architectural docs in prose not contracts. Bus-factor warning.
Pyright configured, 48% coverage, heavy Any/cast. Type checker as decoration not gate.
Composable filter/scorer pipeline for content gating. Applicable to /analyze pipeline.
Browser pool with tiered warmth and janitor lifecycle. 10x memory reduction. JASON-OS infrastructure.
8 lifecycle hooks with clear sequence, auth guidance, security model. Combine with ArchiveBox governance.
Persistent state for long-running tasks. NOTE: on_state_change does NOT exist.
Clean abstract hierarchy with runtime composition via FilterChain.
Docs describe features code implements partially. Verify before recommending.
utils.py at 3,778 lines. Domain functions in one file.

Node script generates 45 workflows from template. Meta-CI pattern for JASON-OS.
Twitter bearer token + Weibo cookie in git. Never do this.
InsecureSkipVerify: true globally. MITM vulnerability.
46 extractors, 23-line CONTRIBUTING, no arch docs.
Pluggable registry for comparing implementations. METHOD_REGISTRY + SCORE_REGISTRY + published dataset. GPL code but pattern extractable.
Clean extract->transform->render separation with 6 extension points and dependency injection.
Auto-discovers components by inspecting module structure. Self-documenting config.
GPL-3.0 blocks adoption. Extract patterns only, never import code.
Shell injection + path traversal + credential exposure in tool processing untrusted PDFs.

notes: Best Docker hardening in T29 corpus. Read-only fs, CAP_DROP ALL, non-root uid 998, alpine, health checks.

candidate: Session pool with per-endpoint rate limiting

Unescaped tweet content via Karax verbatim. Renders untrusted HTML.
Experimental module for parallel parser development. Isolates blast radius of upstream API changes.
No CSP, X-Frame-Options, HSTS in web application. Zero defense-in-depth.
Production MCP server filtering tools by OAuth scopes. 7 tool modules. Uses @modelcontextprotocol/sdk with Zod. JASON-OS Domain 02a reference.
~20 commands in server/commands/ (~8700 lines). Both API routes and MCP tools delegate to same commands. Transport-agnostic business logic layer.
10 hook types, priority ordering, auto-discovery via glob, plugin.json manifests. Structural client/server/shared split per plugin.
Bridge function constructing Koa APIContext from MCP request context. Enables MCP tool reuse of existing API infrastructure.
Sequelize decorator for transparent AES field encryption. Applied at model layer. Relevant to SoNash M4.5 Security & Privacy.
Custom test utility starting real Koa server on port 0. Tests exercise actual transport, middleware, serialization.

SELECT FOR UPDATE in Y.js persistence to prevent concurrent write races. General lesson in collaborative editing safety.

JSON response formatting between models and API/MCP consumers. Per-model presenters.
Base class for async job processors. ~20 specialized implementations for event-driven background work.
POST for everything including reads. Abandons HTTP caching and semantics. Don't regress from SoNash REST-like approach.
All contributor docs externalized. No CONTRIBUTING.md, .env.example, or API docs in repo. SoNash in-repo approach is better.

documents.ts handles ~30 endpoints. Command pattern keeps logic clean but routing layer is discovery burden.
Plugin manifest bundling MCP server + skills for 'claude plugin marketplace add' installation. Enables JASON-OS portability.
Frontmatter declarations enforcing tool access restrictions and preventing auto-invocation on destructive skills.

candidate: Skill without invocation gate on destructive ops
notes: Destructive skills should declare disable-model-invocation. SoNash skills rely on prose guardrails.

Generator yields provider clients in preference order (Jina internal -> Serper Google -> Serper Bing). for...of loop catches per-provider errors and advances. First success wins. Query-shortening fallback strips last N/4 terms up to 4 iterations, RTL-aware.

11+ x-* headers control runtime fetch behavior (x-target-selector, x-wait-for-selector, x-timeout, x-cache-tolerance, x-no-cache, x-respond-with, x-proxy-url, x-set-cookie, x-with-generated-alt, x-engine, x-return-format). Clean separation of URL identity from fetch configuration.

candidate: Tiered fetch fallback chain with sideLoad body hint
notes: cachedScrap() executes: Firestore cache -> curl-impersonate fetch -> (if thin <42 tokens or non-200) proxy retry -> Puppeteer with body passed as sideLoad hint to avoid double-fetch -> jsdom narrowing via Readability -> Turndown format -> stale cache fallback on any throw.

candidate: curl-impersonate as Docker LD_PRELOAD base layer
notes: Two-stage Dockerfile: FROM lwthiker/curl-impersonate:0.6-chrome-slim-bullseye -> FROM node:22. Copy libcurl-impersonate.so from stage 1, set LD_PRELOAD + CURL_IMPERSONATE=chrome116. Every outbound libcurl hop impersonates Chrome 116 TLS fingerprint.

One Docker image deployed 6 times to Cloud Run by varying --args build/stand-alone/<entry>.js at deploy time. Inside container, tsyringe DI resolves the correct API host class (CrawlerHost / SearcherHost / SerpHost) into the same Koa shell. Zero code duplication.

Reference stack: Puppeteer renders -> linkedom parses -> @mozilla/readability narrows to article body -> Turndown (with GFM plugin) emits markdown. All stages swappable via DI. Optional VLM alt-text via OpenAI.

candidate: r.jina.ai hosted service as fetch backend
notes: The running hosted service at https://r.jina.ai/<url> handles SPA, PDFs, JS-rendered pages, cookies, proxies. Free tier + paid. Viable as a fallback fetch backend for /website-analysis in CAS. Adoption cost: one HTTP call. Trade-off: vendor lock-in on a core CAS primitive.

Anti-bot defenses fingerprint the full TLS/TCP stack, not just User-Agent. Real Chrome vs headless Chrome differ at the cipher suite level. curl-impersonate rewrites libcurl to emit Chrome 116's exact fingerprint. Understanding this explains why firecrawl/crawl4ai leak through some sites.

candidate: Commercial OSS without tests as an architectural stance
notes: Reader runs at commercial scale with zero automated tests. Quality floor is TypeScript strict + lint + live observability + traffic volume. Works for the originator (Jina has Cloud Logging + dashboards + traffic); transfers poorly to adopters. File as a cautionary stance, not an emulation target.

candidate: Blog: Reader for search-grounding to improve factuality of LLMs
notes: Jina blog post explaining the rationale for s.jina.ai: LLMs hallucinate less when grounded on actual fetched web content. Search-then-read pattern vs search-only. Thesis alignment with SoNash's deep-research evidence-gathering philosophy.
The only practical multi-URL example using r.jina.ai. Demonstrates URL enumeration, rate limiting, result merging. Useful reference IF SoNash adds a bulk analyze mode to /website-analysis.

README 'How it works' section is literally an image badge linking to deepwiki.com. Zero first-party architecture exposition in a 10.5k-star OSS repo. Do not emulate — own the architecture story in the repo, don't outsource to auto-wiki.

Public repo imports from SSH-only private thinapps-shared submodule containing rate-limit, secrets, DAOs, decorators. README dismisses it as 'not integral' but the code does not compile without it. 'Open source' in license only, not in self-host sense. Erodes adopter trust.
